name: Christmas Pasta Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dried Italian tomatoes, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the porcini mushrooms in warm water for at least 30 minutes. Remove from the water, squeeze dry and chop.
2. In a large saucepan or flameproof casserole, heat the olive oil over moderate heat. Add the carrot, onion, and celery and sauté until the onion is golden, about 4 minutes.
3. Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
4. Add the sausage, ground beef, and pancetta to the pan. Cook over moderate heat, stirring to break up the meat, until the beef and sausage are no longer pink. Drain any excess fat.
5. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
6. Pour in the red wine and cook, stirring occasionally, until it evaporates, about 5 minutes.
7. Add the tomato paste, dried Italian tomatoes, porcini mushrooms, and 1/2 cup of warm water. Simmer for 30 minutes. If the sauce becomes too thick, add a little more water. (The recipe can be prepared up to this point up to 2 days in advance. Cover and refrigerate; reheat before serving.)
8. Add the parsley just before serving.
